key_insight: HotRelax predicts fully relaxed atomic structures in a single forward pass, replacing iterative optimization with an end-to-end high-order tensor neural network.
engineering_relevance: This can greatly speed up high-throughput materials screening and catalytic workflow pipelines by reducing the cost and complexity of structure relaxation.
method: Trains on paired unrelaxed and relaxed structures, not DFT force labels.
Uses high-order tensor message passing to model structural changes.
Outputs a relaxed structure directly, with no iterative inference or post-processing.
evidence_signal: The model was evaluated on five diverse datasets covering 3D bulk crystals, 2D layered materials, and catalysts, and showed strong benchmark performance with low prediction errors and energetically close relaxed structures.
watch_item: As a one-shot relaxation model, performance may depend on how well the training distribution matches the target chemistry and structure classes.
paper_title: High-order tensor neural network for iteration-free structure relaxation
